summary: Split the monolithic item TU `it_266F` into semantic item modules `itspawn`, `itgroundcoll`, `itdraw`, and `itdrop`, with matching build config, split ranges, symbol ownership, and headers. The PR body reports remaining item work at 75 functions and 21 TUs; the automated decomp.dev report showed net progress of +2700 matched code bytes and +5556 linked code bytes, with old `it_266F` broken matches largely mirrored as new matches in the split TUs.
what_changed: Deleted `src/melee/it/it_266F.c` and redistributed its contents into four new TUs: `itspawn.c`, `itgroundcoll.c`, `itdraw.c`, and `itdrop.c`.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` from one `it_266F.c` text range, `0x8026C47C..0x8026F9AC`, to four contiguous ranges: `itspawn` `0x8026C47C..0x8026D564`, `itgroundcoll` `0x8026D564..0x8026EB18`, `itdraw` `0x8026EB18..0x8026F3AC`, and `itdrop` `0x8026F3AC..0x8026F9A0`.
Moved `it_8026F9A0` into `itcoll.c`, added its declaration to `itcoll.h`, and shifted the `itcoll.c` text start from `0x8026F9AC` to `0x8026F9A0`.
Replaced the large prototype list in `it_266F.h` with umbrella includes for `itdraw.h`, `itdrop.h`, `itgroundcoll.h`, and `itspawn.h`.

Added dedicated headers for the split modules, retaining address-prefixed prototype comments such as `/* 26D564 */` and refining some exposed return types, for example `it_8026ECE0` as `u32` and `it_8026E32C` as `s32`.
Updated `configure.py` so `it_266F.c` is no longer built; `itgroundcoll.c` is marked `Matching`, while `itspawn.c`, `itdraw.c`, and `itdrop.c` are marked `NonMatching`.

Reassigned `.rodata`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` ownership after the split: examples include `it_803B8560` now defined in `itcoll.c`, `it_803B8570` and `it_803B857C` in `itmaplib.c`, `it_803B8588` and `it_803B8598` in `it_279C.c`, and `it_803B85A8` in `itzako.c`.

Updated `symbols.txt` for split-local data labels, including adding `...rodata.0` at `0x803B85A8` and changing `it_804DC6A8` and `it_804DC6AC` from global `it_` symbols to local `@304` and `@305` labels.
`itspawn.c` now holds random item spawning and weighted-pick-table code, including functions such as `it_8026C47C`, `it_8026C75C`, `fn_8026C88C`, `it_8026CA4C`, `it_8026D018`, and `it_8026D258`.
`itgroundcoll.c` now holds item ground-collision helpers and callback wrappers, including `it_8026D564` through `it_8026EA9C`, plus several inline or outline helper shims needed for matching.
`itdraw.c` now holds item draw and visibility/TEV-related code, including `it_804D5168`, `it_8026EB18`, `it_8026ECE0`, `it_8026EECC`, and `it_8026EECC_inline_*` helpers.
`itdrop.c` now holds drop/spawn-from-item helpers such as `it_8026F3AC`, `it_8026F3D4`, `it_8026F5C8`, `it_8026F6BC`, `it_8026F7C8`, and `it_8026F8B4`.

`itdosei.c` received a small matching-oriented follow-up: reordered includes, introduced `itDosei_GetJObj`, added a `not_msid_4` local, and adjusted source shape around rotation asserts and dropped/damage code.
smart_moves: The split used contiguous address boundaries in `splits.txt`, which is the right tactic for preserving object order while converting one anonymous item TU into named subsystem files.
Keeping `it_266F.h` as an umbrella header reduced include churn while still letting future work include the more precise `itspawn.h`, `itgroundcoll.h`, `itdraw.h`, or `itdrop.h` directly.
The PR did not leave old rodata as stale externs; it moved constants into the TUs that now own the relevant `.rodata` ranges, which is important for section ordering and matching after a split.
Only `itgroundcoll.c` was marked `Matching`; the other new split files stayed `NonMatching`, avoiding overclaiming even though many functions moved cleanly.
Local symbol treatment was updated in `symbols.txt` instead of preserving misleading global `it_` names for values that became local split artifacts.

The change preserved known matching scaffolding, including `PAD_STACK`, `volatile` locals, `#pragma dont_inline`, and helper inline or outline wrappers, rather than simplifying code in ways that could perturb codegen.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a decomp TU, update code files, headers, build objects, split ranges, and symbol ownership together; partial splits can easily desynchronize linker order from source organization.
A TU split can make decomp.dev report many broken matches in the deleted old unit and many new matches in the new units; interpret that as relocation unless bytes are not recovered elsewhere.
Section ranges may cross semantic boundaries. Here rodata formerly attributed to `it_266F.c` had to be spread into `itcoll`, `itmaplib`, `it_279C`, and `itzako`, not just the four new split modules.
Use a compatibility umbrella header for large splits when many downstream include sites may still refer to the old numbered TU name.
Do not assume guessed helper structs should survive a split; `it_8026F3D4_body_t` was removed from `types.h` once the new `itdrop.c` body used explicit local arrays.

Small matching edits in a nearby item file, such as introducing a trivial getter inline or an otherwise redundant local variable, can be legitimate when they preserve register lifetime or stack shape.
naming_conventions: New item TUs use concise subsystem names: `itspawn`, `itgroundcoll`, `itdraw`, and `itdrop`.
New header guards follow descriptive TU names, such as `GALE01_ITSPAWN_H`, `GALE01_ITGROUNDCOLL_H`, `GALE01_ITDRAW_H`, and `GALE01_ITDROP_H`.
Function symbols remain address-based `it_8026....` names; the PR split modules but did not attempt broad semantic function renaming.
Public prototypes keep address comments like `/* 26F3AC */`, preserving the project's searchable address-to-symbol convention.
Matching-only helpers use local descriptive suffixes tied to the target function, such as `it_8026EECC_inline_1`, `it_8026F3D4_check_kind`, and `itDosei_GetJObj`.
Split-local data labels in `symbols.txt` can use local forms like `@304`, `@305`, and `...rodata.0` instead of global `it_` names when evidence says the symbol is local.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `it_8026CA4C` remains wrapped in `#pragma push` / `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma pop`, showing that the split preserved an existing no-inline requirement.
`itdrop.c` uses explicit padding and a `volatile s32 cnt2` in `it_8026F3D4`, indicating stack and register-shape matching were prioritized over source simplicity.
`itgroundcoll.c` uses a shared inline `it_8026E_inline`, public inline helpers like `it_8026E15C_inline1`, and an outline trampoline chain `it_8026DE98_outline*` to force the desired call structure.
`itdraw.c` uses `it_8026EECC_VARS(ip)` to cast item variable storage and splits draw behavior into `it_8026EECC_inline_0` through `it_8026EECC_inline_3`, preserving repeated codegen shapes.
`itdosei.c` introduces `itDosei_GetJObj` and a `not_msid_4` local, both typical decomp source-shape nudges for matching without changing behavior.
Data-matching required redefining constants in new owning TUs rather than only changing extern declarations; examples include quaternions in `it_279C.c` and zero vectors in `itmaplib.c`.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal review bodies were present in the slice.
The PR body explicitly states the intent: split `it_266F` into `itspawn`, `itgroundcoll`, `itdraw`, and `itdrop`.
The automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 reported matched code 69.42% with +2700 bytes, linked code 41.93% with +5556 bytes, matched data +176 bytes, and linked data +136 bytes.
The bot listed 65 new matches in the new split units and 54 broken matches in the old `it_266F` unit, consistent with relocation from a deleted TU rather than a simple regression.
The automated report highlighted improvements in still-unmatched split items, including `itdraw` `it_8026EECC`, several `itspawn` functions, and `itdrop` `it_8026F3D4`.
follow_up_queries: Which rodata symbols moved when splitting `it_266F`?
Why did `itcoll.c` start at `0x8026F9A0` after PR 2567?
What matching tricks are used in `itgroundcoll.c` for `it_8026E15C`, `it_8026E248`, and `it_8026E32C`?
Why are `it_804DC6A8` and `it_804DC6AC` local labels after the split?
What source-shape edits were made to `itdosei.c` in the same PR?
